company: NYU College of Arts and Science
description: Building GeneCart — AI-powered genomics tooling at NYU.
bullets: Building GeneCart, an AI-assisted genomics discovery platform at NYU's College of Arts and Science.

description: Led engineering of a production Multi-Agent research discovery platform for NYU's research department.
tech: Python, LangGraph, Llama 3.1, FastAPI, Node.js, AWS ECS, Kubernetes, Redis
bullets: Architected a production Multi-Agent Research Discovery Engine (LangGraph + Llama 3.1 70B) that semantically maps global researcher collaboration networks by indexing millions of papers from Elsevier's (Science Direct/Scopus).
Improved scientific query accuracy by 40% with a Multi-Agent Search Router using BGE-M3 dense embeddings and Reciprocal Rank Fusion (RRF).
Sustained 99.9% uptime at 3,000+ RPS using horizontally-scaled async pipeline on AWS ECS with circuit-breaker patterns.
Reduced P99 retrieval latency by 78% (450ms to under 100ms) using Write-Through Redis caching for multi-hop Chain of Retrieval queries.
Cut deployment lead times by 65% owning full CI/CD on Kubernetes with zero-downtime rolling deploys.

description: Engineered a high-concurrency distributed search engine to federate research data from external APIs.
tech: FastAPI, AWS ECS Fargate, AsyncIO, Redis, Docker, Kubernetes
bullets: Built a FastAPI microservice on AWS ECS (Fargate) using AsyncIO to parallelize data fetching from 5+ external APIs, slashing P99 latency from 450ms to under 100ms.
Deployed Redis Write-Through caching layer handling over 3,000 RPS with sub-millisecond retrieval times.
Modernized CI/CD pipelines using Docker and Kubernetes enabling zero-downtime blue-green deployments.

role: TA – Data Structures & Algorithms / Machine Learning for Bioinformatics
description: TA across two graduate courses supporting 50+ students each semester.
bullets: Mentored 50+ graduate students on Distributed System design patterns and algorithmic efficiency.
Collaborated with faculty to design assignments on real-world ML workflows.
Wrote Python scripts to automate grading of Jupyter notebooks, reducing feedback turnaround by 40%.

description: Embedded at Shell PLC building fault-tolerant data infrastructure for global maritime energy logistics.
tech: Java, Apache Kafka, Salesforce, Apex, LWC, PostgreSQL, AWS S3
bullets: Delivered zero data-loss maritime telemetry for Shell PLC via a fault-tolerant Apache Kafka pipeline processing 115GB/day across 200+ offshore crude oil stations in Brazil.
Reduced support ticket volume by 35% across 500+ vendors by launching a Self-Service Portal with automated credential lifecycle management.
Increased system fault tolerance by 39% by decomposing a monolithic Salesforce integration into event-driven Apex Async Micro-functions.
Reduced production incidents by 25% with a defensive ETL validation layer quarantining malformed payloads to an S3 Dead Letter Queue.
Engineered automated workflows for Shell's Self-Help Portal, reducing ITIL service requests by 45%.

description: Built and scaled gradeVITian, a high-traffic PWA for academic grade forecasting.
bullets: Engineered a PHP/MySQL backend scaling to 17K+ monthly active users and 11K+ registered accounts.
Achieved #2 Google Search ranking via programmatic SEO with sub-second mobile load times.
Designed normalized database schemas for high-volume grade calculations.
